task_completion.description: Load the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset in Oracle Analytics Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Oracle Analytics Desktop UI is visible with the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset selected or its columns displayed in the data elements panel.
The visualization type is explicitly set to 'Line' in the grammar panel, or a line chart canvas is visible.

task_completion.description: Configure 'Month' on the X-axis and 'Year' as separate lines
task_completion.visual_evidence: The grammar panel shows 'Month' assigned to the Category (X-axis) section and 'Year' assigned to the Color (Series) section.

task_completion.description: Configure 'Sales' as a cumulative running total on the Y-axis
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Sales' is assigned to the Values (Y-axis) section. There must be evidence of a running total calculation, such as a 'Running Total' or 'Cumulative' option checked in the measure properties, a custom calculation dialog, or the resulting line chart showing strictly upward-trending cumulative lines.

task_completion.description: Save the workbook as 'YTD_Pacing_Analysis' in 'My Folders'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog showing the name 'YTD_Pacing_Analysis' and location 'My Folders', or the application title bar/tab displaying 'YTD_Pacing_Analysis' after saving.

integrity.description: Agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to create the chart
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Oracle Analytics Desktop graphical user interface, rather than terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The chart is genuinely rendered by the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The line chart is actually drawn on the canvas with axes and legends corresponding to the dataset, not an imported image or empty configuration.

integrity.description: The file was saved legitimately through the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The save process is visible in the Oracle Analytics Desktop UI, rather than just renaming an empty file in a file explorer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected exported file format is '.dva' (YTD_Pacing_Analysis.dva). The required columns to be used are 'Month', 'Year', and 'Sales'. The 'Sales' measure must be configured as a running total, which internally uses RSUM/MSUM functions; look for UI indicators of 'Running Total' or 'Cumulative' applied to the Sales measure.
